title: 🧩subworkflow - aws ssm restart linux systemd service on ec2
description:
Subworkflow to restart a systemd service on a Linux EC2 instance via SSM Run Command and verify it is active.

Parameters:

instanceId: (Required) EC2 instance ID
serviceName: (Required) systemd service name (e.g. nginx, httpd, tomcat)
awsRegion: (Required) AWS region
dynatraceawsconnection: (Required) Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection name
guide:
## 🔄 Subworkflow — Restart Linux Systemd Service via SSM

Restarts a named systemd service on a Linux EC2 instance and verifies it reaches 'active' state. Commonly used to recover nginx, httpd, tomcat, or any crashed application service.

### ⚙️ Parameters
- **instanceId** (Required): EC2 instance ID
- **serviceName** (Required): systemd service name
- **awsRegion** (Required): AWS region
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection name

input.Parameters: {"commands": ["systemctl restart {{ input()["serviceName"] }} && sleep 3 && STATUS=$(systemctl is-active {{ input()["serviceName"] }}) && echo \"Service {{ input()["serviceName"] }} status: $STATUS\" && [ \"$STATUS\" = \"active\" ] || (echo \"Service failed to start\" && exit 1)"]}

wait-for-send-command-finish.name: wait-for-send-command-finish
wait-for-send-command-finish.description: Modularize your workflows, run any existing workflow.
